subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Force on charge particle in electric field: F = qE this force will act in direction of electric field. The Charge particle will describe a parabolic path if electric field is acting perpendicular or at some angle with initial direction of motion of particle.
text_html:
Force on charge particle in electric field:
F = qE this force will act in direction of electric field. The Charge particle will describe a parabolic path if electric field is acting perpendicular or at some angle with initial direction of motion of particle.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text:
Magnetic force on charge particle in magnetic field: The force on charge particle moving with velocity {tex}\vec 
u{/tex} in the uniform magnetic field {tex}\vec B{/tex} is given by This is known as magnetic Lorentz force. This force acts perpendicular to the plane containing {tex}\vec 
u{/tex} and {tex}\vec B{/tex} , and is directed as given by right handed screw rule. If the moving charged particle is subjected to a perpendicular magnetic field, it describes a circular path and if moving charged particle is subjected to a magnetic field, making some angle with its initial direction of motion, it will describe a helical path. When {tex}\vec 
u{/tex} is parallel to {tex}\vec B{/tex} , then F = 0 When {tex}\vec 
u{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec B{/tex} , then {tex}\vec F{/tex} is maximum, i.e. F = qvB
text_html:
Magnetic force on charge particle in magnetic field:
The force on charge particle moving with velocity {tex}\vec u{/tex} in the uniform magnetic field {tex}\vec B{/tex} is given by This is known as magnetic Lorentz force. This force acts perpendicular to the plane containing {tex}\vec u{/tex} and {tex}\vec B{/tex}, and is directed as given by right handed screw rule. If the moving charged particle is subjected to a perpendicular magnetic field, it describes a circular path and if moving charged particle is subjected to a magnetic field, making some angle with its initial direction of motion, it will describe a helical path.
When {tex}\vec u{/tex} is parallel to {tex}\vec B{/tex}, then F = 0
When {tex}\vec u{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec B{/tex}, then {tex}\vec F{/tex} is maximum, i.e. F = qvB

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Force on charge particle in simultaneous electric and magnetic field: The total force on charge particle moving in simultaneous electric {tex}\vec E{/tex} and magnetic field {tex}\vec B{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec{F}=\underbrace{q \vec{E}}+\underbrace{q \vec{v} \times \vec{B}}{/tex} This is called Lorentz force equation.
text_html:
Force on charge particle in simultaneous electric and magnetic field:
The total force on charge particle moving in simultaneous electric {tex}\vec E{/tex} and magnetic field {tex}\vec B{/tex}is given by{tex}\vec{F}=\underbrace{q \vec{E}}+\underbrace{q \vec{v} \times \vec{B}}{/tex}
This is called Lorentz force equation.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Force on current carrying conductor in magnetic field: {tex}\vec{F}=I(\vec{l} \times \vec{B}){/tex} Magnitude of the force is F = IIB sin {tex}\theta{/tex} Direction of force {tex}\vec{F}{/tex} is normal to {tex}\vec{l}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{B}{/tex} given by Fleming's left hand rule. If {tex}\theta{/tex} is zero ( {tex}\vec{l}{/tex} is parallel to {tex}\vec{B}{/tex} ) then magnetic force is zero.
text_html:
Force on current carrying conductor in magnetic field:
{tex}\vec{F}=I(\vec{l} \times \vec{B}){/tex}
Magnitude of the force is
F = IIB sin{tex}\theta{/tex}
Direction of force {tex}\vec{F}{/tex} is normal to {tex}\vec{l}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{B}{/tex} given by Fleming's left hand rule. If {tex}\theta{/tex} is zero ({tex}\vec{l}{/tex} is parallel to {tex}\vec{B}{/tex}) then magnetic force is zero.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: SOURCES AND FIELDS Electric field, denoted by {tex} {E} {/tex} , is generated by a charge {tex} Q {/tex} and given by the equation {tex} {E}=Q \hat{{r}} /\left(4 \pi \varepsilon_0\right) r^2 {/tex} . Electric field {tex} {E} {/tex} is vectorial, conveying energy and momentum. Electric fields from multiple charges combine vectorially due to superposition.
text_html:
SOURCES AND FIELDS
Electric field, denoted by {tex} {E} {/tex}, is generated by a charge {tex} Q {/tex} and given by the equation {tex} {E}=Q \hat{{r}} /\left(4 \pi \varepsilon_0\right) r^2 {/tex}.
Electric field {tex} {E} {/tex} is vectorial, conveying energy and momentum.
Electric fields from multiple charges combine vectorially due to superposition.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: MAGNETIC FIELD BASICS Just as static charges produce electric fields, moving charges (currents) generate magnetic fields, symbolized as {tex} {B}({r}) {/tex} . Magnetic fields are vector fields with properties similar to electric fields, obeying the superposition principle.
text_html:
MAGNETIC FIELD BASICS
Just as static charges produce electric fields, moving charges (currents) generate magnetic fields, symbolized as {tex} {B}({r}) {/tex}.
Magnetic fields are vector fields with properties similar to electric fields, obeying the superposition principle.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Lorentz Force In the presence of electric and magnetic fields, a charge {tex} q {/tex} experiences a force {tex} {F}=q[{E}({r})+{v} \times {B}({r})] {/tex} . Lorentz force combines electric and magnetic forces, depending on {tex} q, {v} {/tex} and {tex} {B} {/tex} .
text_html:
Lorentz Force
In the presence of electric and magnetic fields, a charge {tex} q {/tex} experiences a force {tex} {F}=q[{E}({r})+{v} \times {B}({r})] {/tex}.
Lorentz force combines electric and magnetic forces, depending on {tex} q, {v} {/tex} and {tex} {B} {/tex}.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Force on Moving Charges Forces on moving charges obey right-hand rule directions perpendicular to velocity and magnetic field. The force is zero when the charge is not moving; it's nullified when velocity and magnetic field are parallel.
text_html:
Force on Moving Charges
Forces on moving charges obey right-hand rule directions perpendicular to velocity and magnetic field.
The force is zero when the charge is not moving; it's nullified when velocity and magnetic field are parallel.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: UNIT OF MAGNETIC FIELD The magnetic field unit, tesla (T), is determined by the force that a unit charge at unit velocity experiences in the magnetic field. One tesla equals one newton.second/(coulomb ⋅ meter). Smaller unit, gauss, equals {tex} 10^{-4} {/tex} tesla.
text_html:
UNIT OF MAGNETIC FIELD
The magnetic field unit, tesla (T), is determined by the force that a unit charge at unit velocity experiences in the magnetic field.
One tesla equals one newton.second/(coulomb ⋅ meter). Smaller unit, gauss, equals {tex} 10^{-4} {/tex} tesla.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Magnetic Force on Current A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field determined by {tex} {F}=I \boldsymbol{l} \times {B} {/tex} . For a straight conductor, {tex} \boldsymbol{l} {/tex} represents the conductor’s length and aligns with the current direction.
text_html:
Magnetic Force on Current
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field determined by {tex} {F}=I \boldsymbol{l} \times {B} {/tex}.
For a straight conductor, {tex} \boldsymbol{l} {/tex} represents the conductor’s length and aligns with the current direction.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: Conducting Rod Analysis The force on charge carriers in a rod due to a magnetic field is {tex} {F}=(n l A) q {v}_d \times {B} {/tex} . Current density {tex} {j} {/tex} and current {tex} I {/tex} are related as {tex} I l \times {B} {/tex} .
text_html:
Conducting Rod Analysis
The force on charge carriers in a rod due to a magnetic field is {tex} {F}=(n l A) q {v}_d \times {B} {/tex}.
Current density {tex} {j} {/tex} and current {tex} I {/tex} are related as {tex} I l \times {B} {/tex}.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: INTEGRATION IN CONDUCTORS For curved wires, calculate total magnetic force via integration: {tex} \sum\limits_{{j}} \operatorname{Id} \boldsymbol{l}_{{j}} \times {B} {/tex} . This method applies if the wire isn’t straight and can be decomposed into strips.
text_html:
INTEGRATION IN CONDUCTORS
For curved wires, calculate total magnetic force via integration: {tex} \sum\limits_{{j}} \operatorname{Id} \boldsymbol{l}_{{j}} \times {B} {/tex}.
This method applies if the wire isn’t straight and can be decomposed into strips.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: EFFECT OF MAGNETIC FIELDS Magnetic fields do not do work since the force acts perpendicular to velocity, thus not altering the magnitude of speed. Magnetic fields often redirect charged particles without changing their kinetic energy.
text_html:
EFFECT OF MAGNETIC FIELDS
Magnetic fields do not do work since the force acts perpendicular to velocity, thus not altering the magnitude of speed.
Magnetic fields often redirect charged particles without changing their kinetic energy.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Force
text: RELATION BETWEEN $$ E $$ AND $$ B $$ Electric and magnetic fields are intertwined, influencing each other's existence and characteristics. They propagate energy through space and interact cohesively, as consolidated by Maxwell's equations.
text_html:
RELATION BETWEEN $$ E $$ AND $$ B $$
Electric and magnetic fields are intertwined, influencing each other's existence and characteristics.
They propagate energy through space and interact cohesively, as consolidated by Maxwell's equations.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Magnetic Force on Moving Charge The magnetic force acting on a moving charge is always perpendicular to the velocity of the particle. Hence, this force does no work and does not alter the magnitude of the velocity.
text_html:
Magnetic Force on Moving Charge
The magnetic force acting on a moving charge is always perpendicular to the velocity of the particle. Hence, this force does no work and does not alter the magnitude of the velocity.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Comparing Magnetic and Electric Forces Unlike magnetic force, the electric force has the capability to transfer energy since it can have a component parallel or antiparallel to the motion.
text_html:
Comparing Magnetic and Electric Forces
Unlike magnetic force, the electric force has the capability to transfer energy since it can have a component parallel or antiparallel to the motion.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Uniform Magnetic Field Motion In a uniform magnetic field, when the velocity of a charged particle is perpendicular to the magnetic field, it experiences a centripetal force that causes circular motion.
text_html:
Uniform Magnetic Field Motion
In a uniform magnetic field, when the velocity of a charged particle is perpendicular to the magnetic field, it experiences a centripetal force that causes circular motion.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Circular Path Formation Force acting as centripetal force: q v × B Produces circular motion when v and B are perpendicular
text_html:
Circular Path Formation

  • Force acting as centripetal force: q v × B
  • Produces circular motion when v and B are perpendicular

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Helical Motion and Axial Component If velocity also has a component along the field, it remains unchanged, leading to helical motion. The path described is a helix with circular motion perpendicular to the field.
text_html:
Helical Motion and Axial Component
If velocity also has a component along the field, it remains unchanged, leading to helical motion. The path described is a helix with circular motion perpendicular to the field.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Centripetal Force and Radius The magnetic force acts as a centripetal force with a magnitude of qVB . The radius of the circular path is given by r = mv / qB .
text_html:
Centripetal Force and Radius

  • The magnetic force acts as a centripetal force with a magnitude of qVB.
  • The radius of the circular path is given by r = mv / qB.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Angular Frequency in Magnetic Fields The angular frequency for the particle’s circular motion is ω = qB / m , which is independent of its energy and velocity.
text_html:
Angular Frequency in Magnetic Fields
The angular frequency for the particle’s circular motion is ω = qB / m, which is independent of its energy and velocity.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Time of Revolution The time taken for one complete revolution is T = 2π / ω , synonymous with the inverse of the frequency of rotation.
text_html:
Time of Revolution
The time taken for one complete revolution is T = 2π / ω, synonymous with the inverse of the frequency of rotation.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Pitch of the Helix The pitch of the helical motion, which is the distance moved along the field in one rotation, can be calculated using p = 2πmv || / qB .
text_html:
Pitch of the Helix
The pitch of the helical motion, which is the distance moved along the field in one rotation, can be calculated using p = 2πmv|| / qB.

subject: Physics
topic: Motion in a Magnetic Field
text: Cyclotron Design Implications The independence of the angular frequency from energy is significant for designing a cyclotron.
text_html:
Cyclotron Design Implications
The independence of the angular frequency from energy is significant for designing a cyclotron.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Biot-Savart Law: According to this law, the magnitude of magnetic field induction at a point due to a current element of length dl, carrying current I, at a distance r from the current element is given by d {tex}d\vec{B}=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I d \vec{l} \times \vec{r}}{r^3}{/tex} Where {tex}\mu_0{/tex} is permeability of free space. Its value is {tex}4 \pi \times 10^{-7}{/tex} Wb/A-m. The magnitude of magnetic field is B = {tex}B=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I d l \sin \theta}{r^2}{/tex} Where {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between current element Idl and position vector {tex}\vec{r}{/tex} . The direction of magnetic field {tex}\vec {d B}{/tex} is perpendicular to the plane containing {tex}I \vec{d l}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r}{/tex} the direction of {tex}\vec{d B}{/tex} is given by right hand screw rule or right hand thumb rule.
text_html:
Biot-Savart Law:
According to this law, the magnitude of magnetic field induction at a point due to a current element of length dl, carrying current I, at a distance r from the current element is given by d {tex}d\vec{B}=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I d \vec{l} \times \vec{r}}{r^3}{/tex}
Where {tex}\mu_0{/tex} is permeability of free space. Its value is {tex}4 \pi \times 10^{-7}{/tex} Wb/A-m.
The magnitude of magnetic field is
B = {tex}B=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I d l \sin \theta}{r^2}{/tex}

Where {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between current element Idl and position vector {tex}\vec{r}{/tex}. The direction of magnetic field {tex}\vec {d B}{/tex} is perpendicular to the plane containing {tex}I \vec{d l}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r}{/tex} the direction of {tex}\vec{d B}{/tex} is given by right hand screw rule or right hand thumb rule.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Biot-Savart Law The Biot-Savart law describes the magnetic field generated by a current element. The magnetic field (dB) is proportional to the current (I), and the length of the conductor element (dl); inversely proportional to the square of the distance (r) from the point P. The direction of the magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane containing dl and r.
text_html:
Biot-Savart Law
The Biot-Savart law describes the magnetic field generated by a current element.

  • The magnetic field (dB) is proportional to the current (I), and the length of the conductor element (dl); inversely proportional to the square of the distance (r) from the point P.
  • The direction of the magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane containing dl and r.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Vector Expression The vector notation for the Biot-Savart law is: {tex} d {B} \propto \frac{I d \boldsymbol{l} \times {r}}{r^3} {/tex} {tex} =\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I d \boldsymbol{l} \times {r}}{r^3} {/tex}
text_html:
Vector Expression
The vector notation for the Biot-Savart law is:

  • {tex} d {B} \propto \frac{I d \boldsymbol{l} \times {r}}{r^3} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I d \boldsymbol{l} \times {r}}{r^3} {/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Magnitude of Magnetic Field The magnitude of the magnetic field calculated using the Biot-Savart law is given by: {tex} |{d} {B}|=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I {~d} l \sin \theta}{r^2} {/tex} Involves the property of cross product where angle θ is between dL and r.
text_html:
Magnitude of Magnetic Field
The magnitude of the magnetic field calculated using the Biot-Savart law is given by:

  • {tex} |{d} {B}|=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I {~d} l \sin \theta}{r^2} {/tex}
  • Involves the property of cross product where angle θ is between dL and r.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Permeability of Free Space The constant {tex} \mu_0 {/tex} is referred to as the permeability of free space. In SI units, {tex} \mu_0 = 4 \pi \times 10^{-7} {/tex} Tm/A
text_html:
Permeability of Free Space
The constant {tex} \mu_0 {/tex} is referred to as the permeability of free space.

  • In SI units, {tex} \mu_0 = 4 \pi \times 10^{-7} {/tex} Tm/A

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Comparison with Coulomb's Law Both the Biot-Savart Law and Coulomb's Law are long-range interactions and apply the principle of superposition. Electrostatic fields are produced by scalar sources (charges), while magnetic fields are produced by vector sources (current elements).
text_html:
Comparison with Coulomb's Law
  • Both the Biot-Savart Law and Coulomb's Law are long-range interactions and apply the principle of superposition.
  • Electrostatic fields are produced by scalar sources (charges), while magnetic fields are produced by vector sources (current elements).

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Field Directionality Electrostatic fields align with the displacement vector from the source, whereas magnetic fields are perpendicular to the plane of the displacement vector and current element.
text_html:
Field Directionality
  • Electrostatic fields align with the displacement vector from the source, whereas magnetic fields are perpendicular to the plane of the displacement vector and current element.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Angle Dependence The Biot-Savart law accounts for angle dependence in magnetic field strength. Field at an angle where {tex}\theta=0{/tex} , (sinθ=0) results in {tex} |{dB}|=0 {/tex} .
text_html:
Angle Dependence
The Biot-Savart law accounts for angle dependence in magnetic field strength.

  • Field at an angle where {tex}\theta=0{/tex}, (sinθ=0) results in {tex} |{dB}|=0 {/tex} .

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Relation Between Constants There exists a fundamental relationship between {tex}\varepsilon_0{/tex} , the permittivity of free space, {tex}\mu_0{/tex} , the permeability of free space, and the speed of light {tex}c{/tex} . {tex}\varepsilon_0 \mu_0 = \frac{1}{c^2}{/tex}
text_html:
Relation Between Constants
There exists a fundamental relationship between {tex}\varepsilon_0{/tex}, the permittivity of free space, {tex}\mu_0{/tex}, the permeability of free space, and the speed of light {tex}c{/tex}.

  • {tex}\varepsilon_0 \mu_0 = \frac{1}{c^2}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Speed of Light in a Vacuum The product of permeability and permittivity constants is linked with light speed. The speed of light, {tex}c = 3 \times 10^8 \, m/s{/tex} , keeps these constants related: {tex}\varepsilon_0 \mu_0 = \frac{1}{(3 \times 10^8)^2}{/tex} .
text_html:
Speed of Light in a Vacuum
The product of permeability and permittivity constants is linked with light speed.

  • The speed of light, {tex}c = 3 \times 10^8 \, m/s{/tex}, keeps these constants related: {tex}\varepsilon_0 \mu_0 = \frac{1}{(3 \times 10^8)^2}{/tex}.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current Element
text: Fixing Values In SI units, {tex}\mu_0{/tex} is set to a known value, specific to vacuum conditions. Choosing one constant determines the other due to their fixed relation.
text_html:
Fixing Values
In SI units, {tex}\mu_0{/tex} is set to a known value, specific to vacuum conditions.

  • Choosing one constant determines the other due to their fixed relation.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Magnetic field induction at the centre of circular coil of radius r carrying current I: B = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{nI}}{2 \mathrm{r}}{/tex} The direction of magnetic field at the centre is perpendicular to the plane of coil. 5 Magnetic field induction at a point on the axis of a circular coil carrying current is given by: B = {tex}\frac{ \frac {k_0 N}{ a^2}}{2\left(a^2+x^2\right)^{\frac3 2}}{/tex} For N number of turns Where a is the radius of the coil, x is the distance of the point on the axis, from the centre of coil.
text_html:
Magnetic field induction at the centre of circular coil of radius r carrying current I:
B = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{nI}}{2 \mathrm{r}}{/tex}
The direction of magnetic field at the centre is perpendicular to the plane of coil.
5 Magnetic field induction at a point on the axis of a circular coil carrying current is given by:
B = {tex}\frac{ \frac {k_0 N}{ a^2}}{2\left(a^2+x^2\right)^{\frac3 2}}{/tex}
For N number of turns
Where a is the radius of the coil, x is the distance of the point on the axis, from the centre of coil.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Magnetic field induction at a point, at a perpendicular distance 'a' from the axis of long linear conductor carrying current is given by: {tex}\mathrm{B}=\frac{\mu 0}{4 \pi} \frac{2 I}{a}{/tex}
text_html:
Magnetic field induction at a point, at a perpendicular distance 'a' from the axis of long linear conductor carrying current is given by:
{tex}\mathrm{B}=\frac{\mu 0}{4 \pi} \frac{2 I}{a}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Circular Coil Magnetic Field Analysis Evaluation of the magnetic field along the axis of a circular coil involves summing up the effects of infinitely small current elements.
text_html:
Circular Coil Magnetic Field Analysis
Evaluation of the magnetic field along the axis of a circular coil involves summing up the effects of infinitely small current elements.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Steady Current Assumption It is assumed that the current I in the circular loop is steady and the evaluation is done in free space (vacuum).
text_html:
Steady Current Assumption
It is assumed that the current I in the circular loop is steady and the evaluation is done in free space (vacuum).

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Loop Orientation and Magnetic Field Calculation The circular loop is situated in the y-z plane with its center at the origin. The x-axis, which is the loop's axis, helps in calculating the magnetic field at a point P.
text_html:
Loop Orientation and Magnetic Field Calculation
The circular loop is situated in the y-z plane with its center at the origin. The x-axis, which is the loop's axis, helps in calculating the magnetic field at a point P.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Biot-Savart Law Application The magnetic field due to an infinitesimal element of the loop, dB, is computed using Biot-Savart law: Formula: dB= {tex}\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I|d \boldsymbol{l} \times {r}|}{r^3}{/tex}
text_html:
Biot-Savart Law Application
The magnetic field due to an infinitesimal element of the loop, dB, is computed using Biot-Savart law:

  • Formula: dB= {tex}\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I|d \boldsymbol{l} \times {r}|}{r^3}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Displacement Vector and Element Perpendicularity The element of the loop is perpendicular to the displacement vector from it to the axial point. Thus, |d {tex}l \times {r}|=r d{/tex} l .
text_html:
Displacement Vector and Element Perpendicularity
The element of the loop is perpendicular to the displacement vector from it to the axial point. Thus, |d {tex}l \times {r}|=r d{/tex} l.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Resultant Magnetic Field Components When summing over the loop, the components not along the x-axis cancel each other out. Only the component aligned with the x-axis contributes to the field.
text_html:
Resultant Magnetic Field Components
When summing over the loop, the components not along the x-axis cancel each other out. Only the component aligned with the x-axis contributes to the field.

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Integration for Net Contribution The net magnetic field contribution along the x-direction is obtained by integrating over the loop: {d} B_x={d} B \cos \theta where {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{R}{\left(x^2+R^2\right)^{1/2}}{/tex}
text_html:
Integration for Net Contribution
The net magnetic field contribution along the x-direction is obtained by integrating over the loop: {d} B_x={d} B \cos \theta where

  • {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{R}{\left(x^2+R^2\right)^{1/2}}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Total Magnetic Field Formula at P The total magnetic field at point P due to the circular loop is: {tex}B_x \hat{{i}}=\frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2\left(x^2+R^2\right)^{3/2}} \hat{{i}}{/tex}
text_html:
Total Magnetic Field Formula at P
The total magnetic field at point P due to the circular loop is:

  • {tex}B_x \hat{{i}}=\frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2\left(x^2+R^2\right)^{3/2}} \hat{{i}}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Special Case: Magnetic Field at Loop's Center When x=0 (the center of the loop), the magnetic field is simplified to: B_0=\frac{\mu_0 I}{2 R} \hat{{i}}
text_html:
Special Case: Magnetic Field at Loop's Center
When x=0 (the center of the loop), the magnetic field is simplified to:

  • B_0=\frac{\mu_0 I}{2 R} \hat{{i}}

subject: Physics
topic: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
text: Direction of Magnetic Field Lines The right-hand thumb rule gives the direction of the magnetic field: curl the fingers of the right hand along the current's direction, the thumb indicates the field's orientation.
text_html:
Direction of Magnetic Field Lines
The right-hand thumb rule gives the direction of the magnetic field: curl the fingers of the right hand along the current's direction, the thumb indicates the field's orientation.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Ampere's circuital law: It states that the line integral of magnetic field {tex}\vec{B}{/tex} along a closed path is equal to {tex}\mu_0{/tex} times the current(I) passing through the closed path. {tex}\oint \text { B.dl }=\mu_0 \mathbf{I}{/tex}
text_html:
Ampere's circuital law:
It states that the line integral of magnetic field {tex}\vec{B}{/tex} along a closed path is equal to {tex}\mu_0{/tex} times the current(I) passing through the closed path.
{tex}\oint \text { B.dl }=\mu_0 \mathbf{I}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Expression of Biot-Savart Law The Biot-Savart law can be expressed through Ampere's circuital law by considering an open surface with a boundary where currents pass through.
text_html:
Expression of Biot-Savart Law
The Biot-Savart law can be expressed through Ampere's circuital law by considering an open surface with a boundary where currents pass through.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Calculation Using Small Line Elements Magnetic field components at boundary elements are multiplied by their length, summed, and taken to an integral as boundaries become smaller.
text_html:
Calculation Using Small Line Elements
Magnetic field components at boundary elements are multiplied by their length, summed, and taken to an integral as boundaries become smaller.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Ampere's Law Relationship The integral of the magnetic field over a closed loop equals the product of the permeability of space, {tex} \mu_0 {/tex} , and the total current through the surface.
text_html:
Ampere's Law Relationship
The integral of the magnetic field over a closed loop equals the product of the permeability of space, {tex} \mu_0 {/tex}, and the total current through the surface.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Right-hand Rule To apply Ampere's law, use the right-hand rule: curl fingers in the loop's direction; the thumb indicates current direction.
text_html:
Right-hand Rule
To apply Ampere's law, use the right-hand rule: curl fingers in the loop's direction; the thumb indicates current direction.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Simplified Applications In simplified cases, an amperian loop can be selected where magnetic field $$ B $$ is either tangential, normal, or vanishes, simplifying calculations.
text_html:
Simplified Applications
In simplified cases, an amperian loop can be selected where magnetic field $$ B $$ is either tangential, normal, or vanishes, simplifying calculations.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Equation Reduction For part {tex} L {/tex} of the loop with tangential {tex} B {/tex} , Ampere's law simplifies to: {tex} B L=\mu_0 I_e {/tex} .
text_html:
Equation Reduction
For part {tex} L {/tex} of the loop with tangential {tex} B {/tex}, Ampere's law simplifies to: {tex} B L=\mu_0 I_e {/tex}.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Symmetric Systems For symmetric systems, like an infinite current-carrying wire, Ampere’s law aids in magnetic field evaluation similar to Gauss's in electric fields.
text_html:
Symmetric Systems
For symmetric systems, like an infinite current-carrying wire, Ampere’s law aids in magnetic field evaluation similar to Gauss's in electric fields.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Implications of Infinities The magnetic field decreases with distance and remains finite despite the wire being infinite, embodying a direct current-distance relationship.
text_html:
Implications of Infinities
The magnetic field decreases with distance and remains finite despite the wire being infinite, embodying a direct current-distance relationship.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Magnetic Field Lines Lines of magnetic fields from an infinite wire form concentric circles, contrasting with starting and ending lines of electrostatic fields.
text_html:
Magnetic Field Lines
Lines of magnetic fields from an infinite wire form concentric circles, contrasting with starting and ending lines of electrostatic fields.

subject: Physics
topic: Ampere's Circuital Law and its Applications
text: Ampere's Law vs Biot-Savart Law Ampere's circuital law parallels the Biot-Savart law, connecting magnetic field attributes and current effects, but offers simpler calculations for constant currents.
text_html:
Ampere's Law vs Biot-Savart Law
Ampere's circuital law parallels the Biot-Savart law, connecting magnetic field attributes and current effects, but offers simpler calculations for constant currents.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Solenoid: A solenoid consists of an insulated long wire closely wound in the form of a helix. Its length is very large as compared to its diameter. At the axis of long solenoid, carrying a current I B = {tex}\frac {\mu_0 {n I}}{2}{/tex} Where n = number of turns per unit length. Magnetic field at one end of solenoid Bend = {tex}\frac {\mu_0 \mathrm{nI}}{ 2}{/tex}
text_html:
Solenoid: A solenoid consists of an insulated long wire closely wound in the form of a helix. Its length is very large as compared to its diameter.
At the axis of long solenoid, carrying a current I
B = {tex}\frac {\mu_0 {n I}}{2}{/tex}
Where n = number of turns per unit length.
Magnetic field at one end of solenoid Bend = {tex}\frac {\mu_0 \mathrm{nI}}{ 2}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Definition of a Long Solenoid A long solenoid is characterized by having a length significantly larger than its radius.
text_html:
Definition of a Long Solenoid
A long solenoid is characterized by having a length significantly larger than its radius.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Structure of a Solenoid A solenoid consists of a wire wound in the shape of a helix, with each turn acting as a circular loop and turns closely spaced together.
text_html:
Structure of a Solenoid
A solenoid consists of a wire wound in the shape of a helix, with each turn acting as a circular loop and turns closely spaced together.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Net Magnetic Field in a Solenoid The net magnetic field is obtained from the vector sum of the fields due to all the turns in the solenoid.
text_html:
Net Magnetic Field in a Solenoid
The net magnetic field is obtained from the vector sum of the fields due to all the turns in the solenoid.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Insulation of Turns Enamelled wires are used to ensure that each turn is insulated from the others to prevent electrical shorting.
text_html:
Insulation of Turns
Enamelled wires are used to ensure that each turn is insulated from the others to prevent electrical shorting.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Magnetic Field of a Section of a Solenoid In an enlarged view, the circular loops between neighboring turns show cancellation of fields.
text_html:
Magnetic Field of a Section of a Solenoid
In an enlarged view, the circular loops between neighboring turns show cancellation of fields.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Field Inside a Solenoid The magnetic field at the interior mid-point is strong, uniform, and aligned along the solenoid's axis.
text_html:
Field Inside a Solenoid
The magnetic field at the interior mid-point is strong, uniform, and aligned along the solenoid's axis.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Field Outside a Solenoid For an ideal long solenoid, the field outside approaches zero and can be assumed to be zero.
text_html:
Field Outside a Solenoid
For an ideal long solenoid, the field outside approaches zero and can be assumed to be zero.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Amperian Loop in a Solenoid A rectangular Amperian loop can be used to determine the magnetic field in a solenoid. Fields across sections that contribute to it are considered.
text_html:
Amperian Loop in a Solenoid
A rectangular Amperian loop can be used to determine the magnetic field in a solenoid. Fields across sections that contribute to it are considered.

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Calculation of Magnetic Field Inside a Solenoid The field along section ab is represented by {tex} B {/tex} . Using Ampère's Law: {tex} B L = \mu_0 I_e {/tex} The calculated field is {tex} B = \mu_0 n I {/tex}
text_html:
Calculation of Magnetic Field Inside a Solenoid

  1. The field along section ab is represented by {tex} B {/tex}.
  2. Using Ampère's Law: {tex} B L = \mu_0 I_e {/tex}
  3. The calculated field is {tex} B = \mu_0 n I {/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: The Solenoid
text: Application of Solenoids The solenoid is used for creating a uniform magnetic field. Inserting a soft iron core increases the magnetic field strength.
text_html:
Application of Solenoids

  • The solenoid is used for creating a uniform magnetic field.
  • Inserting a soft iron core increases the magnetic field strength.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Magnetic Field Due to a Current There exists a magnetic field due to a conductor carrying a current, which can be described using the Biot-Savart law.
text_html:
Magnetic Field Due to a Current
There exists a magnetic field due to a conductor carrying a current, which can be described using the Biot-Savart law.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Forces Between Current-Carrying Conductors An external magnetic field exerts force on a current-carrying conductor, consistent with the Lorentz force formula.
text_html:
Forces Between Current-Carrying Conductors
An external magnetic field exerts force on a current-carrying conductor, consistent with the Lorentz force formula.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Ampere's Early Studies In the early 19th century, Ampere explored magnetic forces between conductors, focusing on current magnitude, conductors' shape and size, and their relative distances.
text_html:
Ampere's Early Studies
In the early 19th century, Ampere explored magnetic forces between conductors, focusing on current magnitude, conductors' shape and size, and their relative distances.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Parallel Conductors When placed near each other, parallel current-carrying conductors exert magnetic forces on each other.
text_html:
Parallel Conductors
When placed near each other, parallel current-carrying conductors exert magnetic forces on each other.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Magnetic Field Direction The magnetic field due to current 'I_a' in a conductor is downward, as per the right-hand rule, when conductors are aligned horizontally.
text_html:
Magnetic Field Direction
The magnetic field due to current 'I_a' in a conductor is downward, as per the right-hand rule, when conductors are aligned horizontally.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Calculation of Magnetic Field The magnetic field, B_a, at a distance 'd' from a current 'I_a' is calculated as: {tex} B_a=\frac{\mu_0 I_a}{2 \pi d} {/tex} .
text_html:
Calculation of Magnetic Field
The magnetic field, B_a, at a distance 'd' from a current 'I_a' is calculated as:
{tex} B_a=\frac{\mu_0 I_a}{2 \pi d} {/tex}.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Force on a Conductor 'b' Due to 'a' Conductor 'b' feels a force F_ba due to the magnetic field B_a. Its magnitude is: {tex} F_{b a} = \frac{\mu_0 I_a I_b}{2 \pi d} L {/tex} (Equation 4.17).
text_html:
Force on a Conductor 'b' Due to 'a'
Conductor 'b' feels a force F_ba due to the magnetic field B_a. Its magnitude is:
{tex} F_{b a} = \frac{\mu_0 I_a I_b}{2 \pi d} L {/tex} (Equation 4.17).

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Newton's Third Law Consistency The forces between conductors 'a' and 'b' are equal and opposite, verifying Newton's third law: {tex} {F}_{{ba}}=-{F}_{{ab}} {/tex} (Equation 4.18).
text_html:
Newton's Third Law Consistency
The forces between conductors 'a' and 'b' are equal and opposite, verifying Newton's third law:
{tex} {F}_{{ba}}=-{F}_{{ab}} {/tex} (Equation 4.18).

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Current Interaction Rule **Parallel currents attract**, whereas **antiparallel currents repel**; unlike electrostatics where like charges repel.
text_html:
Current Interaction Rule
**Parallel currents attract**, whereas **antiparallel currents repel**; unlike electrostatics where like charges repel.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Definition of Ampere 1 ampere is defined by the force it exerts between two parallel conductors 1 meter apart, producing a force of {tex} 2 \times 10^{-7} {/tex} Newtons per meter.
text_html:
Definition of Ampere
1 ampere is defined by the force it exerts between two parallel conductors 1 meter apart, producing a force of {tex} 2 \times 10^{-7} {/tex} Newtons per meter.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Magnetic Field due to Current A magnetic field is created by a conductor carrying a current, described by the Biot-Savart law.
text_html:
Magnetic Field due to Current
A magnetic field is created by a conductor carrying a current, described by the Biot-Savart law.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Lorentz Force An external magnetic field exerts a force on a current-carrying conductor, following the Lorentz force formula.
text_html:
Lorentz Force
An external magnetic field exerts a force on a current-carrying conductor, following the Lorentz force formula.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Current-Carrying Conductors Interaction Two conductors with current generate magnetic forces on each other, aligning with Ampere's studies on current magnitude, conductor size, shape, and distance.
text_html:
Current-Carrying Conductors Interaction
Two conductors with current generate magnetic forces on each other, aligning with Ampere's studies on current magnitude, conductor size, shape, and distance.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Parallel Conductors Example Two parallel conductors with currents exhibit forces due to established magnetic fields between them.
text_html:
Parallel Conductors Example
Two parallel conductors with currents exhibit forces due to established magnetic fields between them.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Magnetic Field Calculation The magnetic field set by conductor 'a' at 'b' is calculated using: B_a = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 I_a}{2 \pi d}{/tex}
text_html:
Magnetic Field Calculation
The magnetic field set by conductor 'a' at 'b' is calculated using:

  • B_a = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 I_a}{2 \pi d}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Force Between Conductors The conductor 'b' experiences a force due to 'a's magnetic field, calculated as: F_{ba} = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 I_a I_b}{2 \pi d} L{/tex}
text_html:
Force Between Conductors
The conductor 'b' experiences a force due to 'a's magnetic field, calculated as:

  • F_{ba} = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 I_a I_b}{2 \pi d} L{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Newton’s Third Law The forces between the conductors adhere to Newton's third law, demonstrating equal and opposite directions: F_{ba} = -F_{ab}
text_html:
Newton’s Third Law
The forces between the conductors adhere to Newton's third law, demonstrating equal and opposite directions:

  • F_{ba} = -F_{ab}

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Force Direction Between Currents Parallel currents attract, while antiparallel currents repel, contrasting with electrostatics.
text_html:
Force Direction Between Currents
Parallel currents attract, while antiparallel currents repel, contrasting with electrostatics.

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Defining the Ampere The ampere measures the current which results in a defined force between conductors, described as: f_{ba} = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 I_a I_b}{2 \pi d}{/tex}
text_html:
Defining the Ampere
The ampere measures the current which results in a defined force between conductors, described as:

  • f_{ba} = {tex}\frac{\mu_0 I_a I_b}{2 \pi d}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
text: Ampere and Coulomb Relationship The SI unit 'ampere' leads to defining 'coulomb' based on charge flow through a conductor with steady current over time.
text_html:
Ampere and Coulomb Relationship
The SI unit 'ampere' leads to defining 'coulomb' based on charge flow through a conductor with steady current over time.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Torque on coil of area A, having n turns, carrying current I, when suspended in magnetic field of strength B is given by {tex}\tau{/tex} = nIBA sin {tex}\alpha{/tex} where {tex}\alpha{/tex} is the angle which a normal drawn on the plane of the coil makes the direction of magnetic field. Torque is minimum if the plane of coil is perpendicular to the direction of magnetic field, because {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 0 o torque is maximum if the plane of the coil is parallel to the direction of magnetic field, because {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 90 o
text_html:
Torque on coil of area A, having n turns, carrying current I, when suspended in magnetic field of strength B is given by
{tex}\tau{/tex} = nIBA sin{tex}\alpha{/tex}
where {tex}\alpha{/tex} is the angle which a normal drawn on the plane of the coil makes the direction of magnetic field.
Torque is minimum if the plane of coil is perpendicular to the direction of magnetic field, because {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 0o
torque is maximum if the plane of the coil is parallel to the direction of magnetic field, because {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 90o

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Torque on a Rectangular Current Loop A rectangular loop carrying a steady current I in a uniform magnetic field experiences torque but no net force, akin to an electric dipole in a uniform electric field.
text_html:
Torque on a Rectangular Current Loop
A rectangular loop carrying a steady current I in a uniform magnetic field experiences torque but no net force, akin to an electric dipole in a uniform electric field.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Forces on the Loop in the Plane B When placed in the plane of magnetic field B: Forces F1 and F2 are exerted on arms AB and CD. F1 = F2 = I b B. Net force on the loop is zero, but torque exists.
text_html:
Forces on the Loop in the Plane B
When placed in the plane of magnetic field B:
  • Forces F1 and F2 are exerted on arms AB and CD.
  • F1 = F2 = I b B.
  • Net force on the loop is zero, but torque exists.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Torque Calculation The torque τ in the loop is given by: τ = IAB. This represents how forces tend to rotate the loop anticlockwise.
text_html:
Torque Calculation
The torque τ in the loop is given by:
τ = IAB. This represents how forces tend to rotate the loop anticlockwise.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Angle between Loop and Magnetic Field When the loop forms an angle θ: Forces on BC and DA cancel each other. Torque is reduced as distance between the forces decreases. Torque τ is given by: τ = IAB sin(θ).
text_html:
Angle between Loop and Magnetic Field
When the loop forms an angle θ:

  • Forces on BC and DA cancel each other.
  • Torque is reduced as distance between the forces decreases.

Torque τ is given by:
τ = IAB sin(θ).

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Magnetic Moment of the Loop Magnetic moment m is determined as: m = IA. The direction is by the right-hand thumb rule and into the plane of the paper.
text_html:
Magnetic Moment of the Loop
Magnetic moment m is determined as:
m = IA. The direction is by the right-hand thumb rule and into the plane of the paper.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Expression for Torque using Magnetic Moment The torque can be expressed as a vector product: τ = m × B. This is analogous to equations in electrostatics with dipole moment pe and electric field E.
text_html:
Expression for Torque using Magnetic Moment
The torque can be expressed as a vector product:
τ = m × B. This is analogous to equations in electrostatics with dipole moment pe and electric field E.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Equilibrium Conditions When m is parallel/antiparallel to B: No torque exists, indicating equilibrium. Parallel positions are stable; any slight rotation corrects back. Antiparallel is unstable; rotation increases torque.
text_html:
Equilibrium Conditions
When m is parallel/antiparallel to B:
  • No torque exists, indicating equilibrium.
  • Parallel positions are stable; any slight rotation corrects back.
  • Antiparallel is unstable; rotation increases torque.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Torque with Multiple Turns For a loop with N turns, the equation for torque remains applicable: m = NIA. The formula accounts for layered winding.
text_html:
Torque with Multiple Turns
For a loop with N turns, the equation for torque remains applicable:
m = NIA. The formula accounts for layered winding.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Circular Current Loop as a Magnetic Dipole The magnetic field from a current loop behaves similarly to an electric field of a dipole: Field strength B = (μ0m) / (2πx³). Analogy with electrostatic field exists: μ0 → (1/ε0), m → pe, B → E.
text_html:
Circular Current Loop as a Magnetic Dipole
The magnetic field from a current loop behaves similarly to an electric field of a dipole:

  • Field strength B = (μ0m) / (2πx³).
  • Analogy with electrostatic field exists: μ0 → (1/ε0), m → pe, B → E.

subject: Physics
topic: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
text: Planar Loop as a Magnetic Dipole A planar current loop is a magnetic dipole with moment m = IA. Unlike electric dipoles, magnetic dipoles don't consist of monopoles, implying fundamental differences in nature.
text_html:
Planar Loop as a Magnetic Dipole
A planar current loop is a magnetic dipole with moment m = IA. Unlike electric dipoles, magnetic dipoles don't consist of monopoles, implying fundamental differences in nature.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Radial magnetic field: It is magnetic field in which plane of coil in all positions remain parallel to the direction of magnetic field.
text_html: Radial magnetic field: It is magnetic field in which plane of coil in all positions remain parallel to the direction of magnetic field.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Moving coil galvanometer: A moving coil galvanometer is a device used to detect flow of current in a circuit. A moving coil galvanometer consists of a rectangular coil placed in a uniform radial magnetic field produced by cylindrical pole pieces. Torque on coil {tex}\tau{/tex} = nIBA where n is number of turns, A is area of coil. If k is torsional constant of material of suspended wire Then for deflection {tex}\theta{/tex} torque is For equilibrium nIBA= k {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\theta = \frac {nIBA}{k}{/tex} Clearly deflection in galvanometer is directly proportional to current.
text_html:
Moving coil galvanometer:
A moving coil galvanometer is a device used to detect flow of current in a circuit. A moving coil galvanometer consists of a rectangular coil placed in a uniform radial magnetic field produced by cylindrical pole pieces. Torque on coil {tex}\tau{/tex} = nIBA where n is number of turns, A is area of coil. If k is torsional constant of material of suspended wire
Then for deflection {tex}\theta{/tex} torque is For equilibrium nIBA= k{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\theta = \frac {nIBA}{k}{/tex}
Clearly deflection in galvanometer is directly proportional to current.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Current sensitivity of galvanometer: It is defined as deflection produced in galvanometer, when unit current flows through it. {tex}\frac{\theta}{I}=\frac{n B A}{k}{/tex} Unit of current sensitivity is rad A -1 or div A -1
text_html:
Current sensitivity of galvanometer:
It is defined as deflection produced in galvanometer, when unit current flows through it.
{tex}\frac{\theta}{I}=\frac{n B A}{k}{/tex}
Unit of current sensitivity is rad A-1 or div A-1

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Voltage sensitivity of galvanometer: It is defined as deflection produced in galvanometer when unit voltage is applied across two terminals of galvanometer. {tex}\frac{\theta}{V}=\frac{n B A}{k R}{/tex} Unit of voltage sensitivity is rad.V -1 or div.V -1
text_html:
Voltage sensitivity of galvanometer:
It is defined as deflection produced in galvanometer when unit voltage is applied across two terminals of galvanometer.
{tex}\frac{\theta}{V}=\frac{n B A}{k R}{/tex}
Unit of voltage sensitivity is rad.V-1 or div.V-1

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Shunt: It is a low resistance wire connected in parallel with galvanometer or ammeter. It protects the galvanometer or ammeter from strong currents.
text_html: Shunt: It is a low resistance wire connected in parallel with galvanometer or ammeter. It protects the galvanometer or ammeter from strong currents.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Conversion of galvanometer into ammeter: A galvanometer can be converted into ammeter by using very small resistance in parallel the galvanometer coil. The small resistance connected in parallel is called a shunt. If G is resistance of galvanometer, Ig is current in galvanometer for full-scale deflection, then for conversion of galvanometer into an ammeter of range I ampere, the shunt is given by S = {tex}\frac {IgG}{Ig}{/tex}
text_html:
Conversion of galvanometer into ammeter:
A galvanometer can be converted into ammeter by using very small resistance in parallel the galvanometer coil. The small resistance connected in parallel is called a shunt. If G is resistance of galvanometer, Ig is current in galvanometer for full-scale deflection, then for conversion of galvanometer into an ammeter of range I ampere, the shunt is given by S = {tex}\frac {IgG}{Ig}{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Conversion of galvanometer into voltmeter: A galvanometer can be converted into voltmeter by connecting high resistance (R) in series with the coil of galvanometer. If V volt is the range of voltmeter formed, then series resistance is given by R = {tex}(\frac {V}{Ig})-G{/tex}
text_html:
Conversion of galvanometer into voltmeter: A galvanometer can be converted into voltmeter by connecting high resistance (R) in series with the coil of galvanometer. If V volt is the range of voltmeter formed, then series resistance is given by
R = {tex}(\frac {V}{Ig})-G{/tex}

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Measuring Current and Voltage In circuits, measuring current and voltage is essential. Instruments such as the moving coil galvanometer (MCG) are used for this purpose, which can be adapted for use as either an ammeter or voltmeter based on requirements.
text_html:
Measuring Current and Voltage
In circuits, measuring current and voltage is essential. Instruments such as the moving coil galvanometer (MCG) are used for this purpose, which can be adapted for use as either an ammeter or voltmeter based on requirements.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Moving Coil Galvanometer Elements The MCG features a freely rotating coil within a uniform radial magnetic field. A cylindrical soft iron core increases field strength, where current-induced torque acts on the coil.
text_html:
Moving Coil Galvanometer Elements
The MCG features a freely rotating coil within a uniform radial magnetic field. A cylindrical soft iron core increases field strength, where current-induced torque acts on the coil.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Torque Calculation The torque acting on the coil is calculated using: • {tex}\tau=N I A B{/tex} • Balance is achieved when counter-torque by a spring equals magnetic torque.
text_html:
Torque Calculation
The torque acting on the coil is calculated using:
• {tex}\tau=N I A B{/tex}
• Balance is achieved when counter-torque by a spring equals magnetic torque.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Current Detection The galvanometer is used for current detection. It serves in setups like Wheatstone’s bridge, showing deflection based on current flow.
text_html:
Current Detection
The galvanometer is used for current detection. It serves in setups like Wheatstone’s bridge, showing deflection based on current flow.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Conversion to Ammeter An MCG cannot directly measure current due to sensitivity and resistance. A shunt resistance {tex}r_s{/tex} , with low value, is attached in parallel to allow most of the current to pass through the shunt, minimizing disturbance.
text_html:
Conversion to Ammeter
An MCG cannot directly measure current due to sensitivity and resistance. A shunt resistance {tex}r_s{/tex}, with low value, is attached in parallel to allow most of the current to pass through the shunt, minimizing disturbance.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Ammeter Resistance Arrangement The resistance of the ammeter setup is: • {tex}R_G r_s / (R_G + r_s) \approx r_s{/tex} • Ensures minimal disturbance when introduced in a circuit.
text_html:
Ammeter Resistance Arrangement
The resistance of the ammeter setup is:
• {tex}R_G r_s / (R_G + r_s) \approx r_s{/tex}
• Ensures minimal disturbance when introduced in a circuit.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Current Sensitivity The galvanometer's current sensitivity is defined as the deflection per unit current: • {tex}\frac{\phi}{I} = \frac{NAB}{k}{/tex} • More turns {tex}N{/tex} increase sensitivity.
text_html:
Current Sensitivity
The galvanometer's current sensitivity is defined as the deflection per unit current:
• {tex}\frac{\phi}{I} = \frac{NAB}{k}{/tex}
• More turns {tex}N{/tex} increase sensitivity.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Voltmeter Connection A galvanometer converts to a voltmeter with a large resistance {tex}R{/tex} in series, requiring: • Parallel connection to circuit section • Minimal disturbance to setup, usually below 1%.
text_html:
Voltmeter Connection
A galvanometer converts to a voltmeter with a large resistance {tex}R{/tex} in series, requiring:
• Parallel connection to circuit section
• Minimal disturbance to setup, usually below 1%.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Voltage Sensitivity Voltage sensitivity, or deflection per unit voltage, is: • {tex}\frac{\phi}{V} = \frac{NAB}{k} \times \frac{1}{R}{/tex} • Resistance increase can neutralize turn increase effects on voltage sensitivity.
text_html:
Voltage Sensitivity
Voltage sensitivity, or deflection per unit voltage, is:
• {tex}\frac{\phi}{V} = \frac{NAB}{k} \times \frac{1}{R}{/tex}
• Resistance increase can neutralize turn increase effects on voltage sensitivity.

subject: Physics
topic: The Moving Coil Galvanometer, Voltmeter and ammeter
text: Modifications for Ammeter and Voltmeter Differing needs for ammeter and voltmeter conversion: • Ammeter needs a shunt resistance in parallel. • Voltmeter requires a large resistance in series.
text_html:
Modifications for Ammeter and Voltmeter
Differing needs for ammeter and voltmeter conversion:
• Ammeter needs a shunt resistance in parallel.
• Voltmeter requires a large resistance in series.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Historical Background of Electricity and Magnetism Both electricity and magnetism were known over 2000 years ago but their interconnection was understood only in 1820.
text_html:
Historical Background of Electricity and Magnetism
Both electricity and magnetism were known over 2000 years ago but their interconnection was understood only in 1820.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Oersted's Discovery Hans Christian Oersted first discovered the relationship between electricity and magnetism during a lecture demonstration by observing a current's effect on a compass needle.
text_html:
Oersted's Discovery
Hans Christian Oersted first discovered the relationship between electricity and magnetism during a lecture demonstration by observing a current's effect on a compass needle.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Magnetic Field around a Current-Carrying Wire Current in a straight wire causes a compass needle to deflect, aligning tangentially to an imaginary circle around the wire.
text_html:
Magnetic Field around a Current-Carrying Wire
Current in a straight wire causes a compass needle to deflect, aligning tangentially to an imaginary circle around the wire.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Behavior of Compass Needles Needle orientation reverses if current direction is reversed. Deflection increases with higher current or closer proximity to the wire.
text_html:
Behavior of Compass Needles
  • Needle orientation reverses if current direction is reversed.
  • Deflection increases with higher current or closer proximity to the wire.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Arrangement of Iron Filings Iron filings around a current-carrying wire arrange into concentric circles indicating the magnetic field produced by the wire.
text_html:
Arrangement of Iron Filings
Iron filings around a current-carrying wire arrange into concentric circles indicating the magnetic field produced by the wire.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Magnetic Fields from Moving Charges Oersted's experiments led to the conclusion that moving charges or currents produce magnetic fields.
text_html:
Magnetic Fields from Moving Charges
Oersted's experiments led to the conclusion that moving charges or currents produce magnetic fields.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Maxwell's Unification In 1864, James Maxwell unified the laws of electricity and magnetism and realized that light consisted of electromagnetic waves.
text_html:
Maxwell's Unification
In 1864, James Maxwell unified the laws of electricity and magnetism and realized that light consisted of electromagnetic waves.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Discovery of Radio Waves Radio waves were first discovered by Hertz and later produced by J.C. Bose and G. Marconi at the end of the 19th century.
text_html:
Discovery of Radio Waves
Radio waves were first discovered by Hertz and later produced by J.C. Bose and G. Marconi at the end of the 19th century.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Electromagnetic Wave Devices The 20th century marked significant scientific progress with the invention of devices for producing, amplifying, transmitting, and detecting electromagnetic waves.
text_html:
Electromagnetic Wave Devices
The 20th century marked significant scientific progress with the invention of devices for producing, amplifying, transmitting, and detecting electromagnetic waves.

subject: Physics
topic: Introduction
text: Diagram Interpretation The given diagram illustrates the magnetic field around a wire with compass needles and iron filings to show orientation when current moves through the plane or into it.
text_html:
Diagram Interpretation

  • The given diagram illustrates the magnetic field around a wire with compass needles and iron filings to show orientation when current moves through the plane or into it.
